content.problemStatement:
You are given three binary strings†^\dagger† S1,S2,S3S_{1}, S_{2}, S_{3}S1​,S2​,S3​ each of length NNN. Your goal is to make each string contain identical characters (all 0′s0's0′s or all 1′s1's1′s).

To achieve this, you can perform the following operation:


Choose x,yx, yx,y from {1,2,3}\{1, 2, 3\}{1,2,3}.
Choose index i,ji, ji,j such that 0≤i,j<N0 \leq i, j \lt N0≤i,j<N.
Swap SxiS_{xi}Sxi​ and SyjS_{yj}Syj​ (character at ithi^{th}ith index in SxS_{x}Sx​ and character at jthj^{th}jth index in SyS_{y}Sy​).
Find the **minimum** number of operations required to make each string contain identical characters. Print −1-1−1 if not possible.

†^\dagger†A **binary string** is a sequence of characters where each character is either ′0′'0'′0′ or ′1′'1'′1′.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN, the length of the string.
The next 333 lines of input contain 333 binary strings.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of operations to required to make all strings have same characters, −1-1−1 if it is not possible.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases ≤105\leq 10^5≤105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: Swap 0th0^{th}0th character of S1S_1S1​ and 0th0^{th}0th character of S3S_3S3​ (zero based indexing).
Test case 2: Swap 1st1^{st}1st character of S1S_1S1​ and 0th0^{th}0th character of S3S_3S3​.
Test case 3: It can be shown that no sequence of allowed operations can make all three strings contain the same character simultaneously.
Test case 4: All strings already contain the same characters.
